name: Prefix and Suffix Products
summary: Fill each answer cell with the running product of everything to its left, then walk back multiplying in the running product of everything to its right.
visualizationIntent: init: an answer array of all ones pops in beside the input. forward pass: a pointer sweeps left to right writing the running prefix product into each cell, so cells to the left settle as visited. suffix pass: a pointer sweeps right to left multiplying each cell by the running suffix product, turning finalized cells result-green from the right. return: every cell now holds the product of all other elements.

lineExplanations.3: Store the length of the array.
lineExplanations.4: Create the answer array filled with ones, one slot per element.
lineExplanations.5: Start the running prefix product at one.
lineExplanations.6: Sweep left to right across every index.
lineExplanations.7: Write the product of everything to the left into this cell.
lineExplanations.8: Fold the current value into the running prefix product for later cells.
lineExplanations.9: Start the running suffix product at one.
lineExplanations.10: Sweep right to left across every index.
lineExplanations.11: Multiply this cell by the product of everything to its right.
lineExplanations.12: Fold the current value into the running suffix product for earlier cells.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper expected by the platform.
syntaxExplanations.2: def productExceptSelf(self, nums): defines the method receiving the array nums.
syntaxExplanations.3: n = len(nums) stores how many elements are in nums.
syntaxExplanations.4: answer = [1] * n builds a list of n ones.
syntaxExplanations.5: prefix = 1 starts the left-to-right running product at one.
syntaxExplanations.6: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 up to n - 1.
syntaxExplanations.7: answer[i] = prefix stores the current prefix product in this cell.
syntaxExplanations.8: prefix = prefix * nums[i] multiplies the current value into the prefix.
syntaxExplanations.9: suffix = 1 starts the right-to-left running product at one.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1): loops i from n - 1 down to 0.
syntaxExplanations.11: answer[i] = answer[i] * suffix multiplies the stored prefix by the suffix.
syntaxExplanations.12: suffix = suffix * nums[i] multiplies the current value into the suffix.
syntaxExplanations.13: return answer sends back the finished array.
